option.name: originalMessage
option.value:
add observability instrumentation across agent pipeline
For every tool call and every LLM API call, the system now captures and records timing, size, token usage, and estimated cost.
src/llm.py - LLM.__init__ gains call_log: list[dict] (one entry per complete() call) - LLM.complete() wraps the API call with time.monotonic() and appends a span containing: ok, duration_ms, model, input_chars, output_chars, input_tokens, output_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, error (on failure) - Token usage is extracted from resp.usage safely via getattr (None if absent) - _estimate_cost() looks up per-million-token rates for known OpenAI models (gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1-nano, gpt-4.1, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini)
src/agent_state.py - add_tool_history() accepts three new optional kwargs: duration_ms, input_chars, output_chars — stored in the tool_history entry when provided
src/agent_loop.py - All tool dispatch paths now record duration_ms and input_chars; successful paths also record output_chars - Covers: generate_target_resume dispatch, generic TOOLS[] dispatch, and both fallback generate_target_resume calls (premature-final guard + max-iters)
src/observability.py (new) - build_run_artifact(run_id, llm, state) assembles the run artifact dict: run_id, model, tools (spans from tool_history), llm_calls (from llm.call_log), totals (summed duration_ms, llm_call_count, estimated_cost_usd)
src/run_evals.py - _run_case() now accepts run_id, calls build_run_artifact(), and includes the result in its return dict - _save_case_outputs() writes run_artifact.json alongside other per-case files - main() generates run_id = "<timestamp>_<slug>" per case before dispatching
tests/test_agent_loop.py - Updated test_run_agent_happy_path: the premature-final fallback now records its failed generate_target_resume attempt in tool_history, so expected count is 4 (3 successful + 1 failed fallback); test now asserts on this
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

option.name: rewordedMessage
option.value:
add observability instrumentation across agent pipeline
For every tool call and every LLM API call, the system now captures and records timing, size, token usage, and estimated cost.
src/llm.py - LLM.__init__ gains call_log: list[dict] (one entry per complete() call) - LLM.complete() wraps the API call with time.monotonic() and appends a span containing: ok, duration_ms, model, input_chars, output_chars, input_tokens, output_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, error (on failure) - Token usage is extracted from resp.usage safely via getattr (None if absent) - _estimate_cost() looks up per-million-token rates for known OpenAI models (gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1-nano, gpt-4.1, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini)
src/agent_state.py - add_tool_history() accepts three new optional kwargs: duration_ms, input_chars, output_chars — stored in the tool_history entry when provided
src/agent_loop.py - All tool dispatch paths now record duration_ms and input_chars; successful paths also record output_chars - Covers: generate_target_resume dispatch, generic TOOLS[] dispatch, and both fallback generate_target_resume calls (premature-final guard + max-iters)
src/observability.py (new) - build_run_artifact(run_id, llm, state) assembles the run artifact dict: run_id, model, tools (spans from tool_history), llm_calls (from llm.call_log), totals (summed duration_ms, llm_call_count, estimated_cost_usd)
src/run_evals.py - _run_case() now accepts run_id, calls build_run_artifact(), and includes the result in its return dict - _save_case_outputs() writes run_artifact.json alongside other per-case files - main() generates run_id = "<timestamp>_<slug>" per case before dispatching
tests/test_agent_loop.py - Updated test_run_agent_happy_path: the premature-final fallback now records its failed generate_target_resume attempt in tool_history, so expected count is 4 (3 successful + 1 failed fallback); test now asserts on this
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
update README for observability addition
- Add observability.py to project structure - Add run_artifact.json to per-case eval output file list
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

task.summary: agent_state.py - tighter v2 with these upgrades: - typed action objects instead of ad hoc dicts - stronger, more domain-specific tools - safer JSON parsing and validation - clearer intermediate artifacts - friendlier OpenAI error handling - a deterministic markdown renderer tool

llm.py - tighter v2 with these upgrades: - typed action objects instead of ad hoc dicts - stronger, more domain-specific tools - safer JSON parsing and validation - clearer intermediate artifacts - friendlier OpenAI error handling - a deterministic markdown renderer tool

tools.py - tighter v2 with these upgrades: - typed action objects instead of ad hoc dicts - stronger, more domain-specific tools - safer JSON parsing and validation - clearer intermediate artifacts - friendlier OpenAI error handling - a deterministic markdown renderer tool

tools.py - tighter v2 with these upgrades: - typed action objects instead of ad hoc dicts - stronger, more domain-specific tools - safer JSON parsing and validation - clearer intermediate artifacts - friendlier OpenAI error handling - a deterministic markdown renderer tool

tools.py - tighter v2 with these upgrades: - typed action objects instead of ad hoc dicts - stronger, more domain-specific tools - safer JSON parsing and validation - clearer intermediate artifacts - friendlier OpenAI error handling - a deterministic markdown renderer tool
add separate folder for sample job specs

current _REQUIREMENT_HINTS set does not include some Netflix-important terms like: - python - java - api - apis - genai - llm - workflow - integrations
Right now the extractor may still find some of these if frequency and token length help, but it would be better to add them explicitly because they are central to the job spec.
The posting explicitly asks for backend technologies, GenAI/LLMs, Python and/or Java, APIs, integrations, and cloud platforms.
revise test fixture for netflix swe5 role
revise test fixture for netflix swe5 role
upgrade that makes requirement extraction noticeably better for Netflix-style backend / internal-platform / GenAI-adjacent roles, while keeping the project simple and deterministic
update keyword list to be slightly more phrase-oriented
adding "resume_corpus" folder containing: 1. job specs 2. drafted resumes 3. original, raw-data resume
agent_state.py - store corpus examples and the generated resume
agent_state.py - store corpus examples and the generated resume
tools.py - corpus loading and markdown normalization
tools.py - Score and retrieve similar examples

collapse into single workflow for simplicity (i.e. the one that uses resume corpus) removes: - desired_output - report-vs-resume branching - report fallback logic from the loop
agent now has exactly one job: - generate the targeted resume agent also directly invokes 'report rendering'

For every generated resume, add a second LLM call that scores it on: -JD alignment - keyword coverage - clarity - exaggeration risk - ATS compatibility And returns: - numeric scores - brief rationale - concrete revision suggestions
start to update readme for new functionality

MESSAGE.value: revise test fixture for netflix swe5 role
upgrade that makes requirement extraction noticeably better for Netflix-style backend / internal-platform / GenAI-adjacent roles, while keeping the project simple and deterministic
update keyword list to be slightly more phrase-oriented
adding "resume_corpus" folder containing: 1. job specs 2. drafted resumes 3. original, raw-data resume
agent_state.py - store corpus examples and the generated resume
tools.py - corpus loading and markdown normalization
tools.py - Score and retrieve similar examples

collapse into single workflow for simplicity (i.e. the one that uses resume corpus) removes: - desired_output - report-vs-resume branching - report fallback logic from the loop
agent now has exactly one job: - generate the targeted resume agent also directly invokes 'report rendering'

For every generated resume, add a second LLM call that scores it on: -JD alignment - keyword coverage - clarity - exaggeration risk - ATS compatibility And returns: - numeric scores - brief rationale - concrete revision suggestions
start to update readme for new functionality

add evaluation harness: batch runner and three eval cases
src/run_evals.py — new batch evaluation runner - Discovers eval cases under --eval-dir (any subdir with jd.txt + raw_resume.txt) - Runs the full agent pipeline per case: load corpus → extract requirements → retrieve similar examples → generate targeted resume - Applies post-loop safety net matching agent.py:main(): fit scoring, LLM evaluation, and report rendering - Saves all artifacts per case to --out-dir/<slug>/ target_resume.txt, report.md, requirements.json, retrieved_examples.json, evaluation.json, run_metadata.json - Validates extracted requirements against expected_requirements.json (min_keyword_matches threshold) when present - Writes a summary JSON to evals/summaries/summary_<timestamp>.json with: aggregate averages for overall score, JD alignment, keyword coverage, exaggeration safety, ATS compatibility, and deterministic fit score; retrieval hit rate; failed run count; requirement validation pass/fail
evals/cases/ — three bootstrapped eval cases - netflix_swe_n_tech: internal SWE role, GenAI/LLM/Python/cloud focus - google_cloud_ai_ml: Cloud AI Engineer, ML/GCP/Python/data pipelines focus - databricks_ml_ai_finserv: ML/AI SSA, LLM/MLOps/cloud/data focus Each case includes jd.txt, raw_resume.txt, and expected_requirements.json
evals/outputs/ and evals/summaries/ tracked via .gitkeep
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
update README for evaluation harness addition
- Add run_evals.py to project structure section - Add evals/ directory layout with cases, outputs, and summaries - Add batch evaluation usage example with correct CLI flags (also fixes single-run flags: --out-resume / --out-report) - Add Batch Evaluation Harness section under Key Concepts with per-case output file list and summary metrics table - Add evaluation harness to the intro bullet list - Update Possible Extensions to reflect what is now built
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add observability instrumentation across agent pipeline
For every tool call and every LLM API call, the system now captures and records timing, size, token usage, and estimated cost.
src/llm.py - LLM.__init__ gains call_log: list[dict] (one entry per complete() call) - LLM.complete() wraps the API call with time.monotonic() and appends a span containing: ok, duration_ms, model, input_chars, output_chars, input_tokens, output_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, error (on failure) - Token usage is extracted from resp.usage safely via getattr (None if absent) - _estimate_cost() looks up per-million-token rates for known OpenAI models (gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1-nano, gpt-4.1, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini)
src/agent_state.py - add_tool_history() accepts three new optional kwargs: duration_ms, input_chars, output_chars — stored in the tool_history entry when provided
src/agent_loop.py - All tool dispatch paths now record duration_ms and input_chars; successful paths also record output_chars - Covers: generate_target_resume dispatch, generic TOOLS[] dispatch, and both fallback generate_target_resume calls (premature-final guard + max-iters)
src/observability.py (new) - build_run_artifact(run_id, llm, state) assembles the run artifact dict: run_id, model, tools (spans from tool_history), llm_calls (from llm.call_log), totals (summed duration_ms, llm_call_count, estimated_cost_usd)
src/run_evals.py - _run_case() now accepts run_id, calls build_run_artifact(), and includes the result in its return dict - _save_case_outputs() writes run_artifact.json alongside other per-case files - main() generates run_id = "<timestamp>_<slug>" per case before dispatching
tests/test_agent_loop.py - Updated test_run_agent_happy_path: the premature-final fallback now records its failed generate_target_resume attempt in tool_history, so expected count is 4 (3 successful + 1 failed fallback); test now asserts on this
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
update README for observability addition
- Add observability.py to project structure - Add run_artifact.json to per-case eval output file list
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value:
add observability instrumentation across agent pipeline
For every tool call and every LLM API call, the system now captures and records timing, size, token usage, and estimated cost.
src/llm.py - LLM.__init__ gains call_log: list[dict] (one entry per complete() call) - LLM.complete() wraps the API call with time.monotonic() and appends a span containing: ok, duration_ms, model, input_chars, output_chars, input_tokens, output_tokens, estimated_cost_usd, error (on failure) - Token usage is extracted from resp.usage safely via getattr (None if absent) - _estimate_cost() looks up per-million-token rates for known OpenAI models (gpt-4.1-mini, gpt-4.1-nano, gpt-4.1, gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini)
src/agent_state.py - add_tool_history() accepts three new optional kwargs: duration_ms, input_chars, output_chars — stored in the tool_history entry when provided
src/agent_loop.py - All tool dispatch paths now record duration_ms and input_chars; successful paths also record output_chars - Covers: generate_target_resume dispatch, generic TOOLS[] dispatch, and both fallback generate_target_resume calls (premature-final guard + max-iters)
src/observability.py (new) - build_run_artifact(run_id, llm, state) assembles the run artifact dict: run_id, model, tools (spans from tool_history), llm_calls (from llm.call_log), totals (summed duration_ms, llm_call_count, estimated_cost_usd)
src/run_evals.py - _run_case() now accepts run_id, calls build_run_artifact(), and includes the result in its return dict - _save_case_outputs() writes run_artifact.json alongside other per-case files - main() generates run_id = "<timestamp>_<slug>" per case before dispatching
tests/test_agent_loop.py - Updated test_run_agent_happy_path: the premature-final fallback now records its failed generate_target_resume attempt in tool_history, so expected count is 4 (3 successful + 1 failed fallback); test now asserts on this
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
update README for observability addition
- Add observability.py to project structure - Add run_artifact.json to per-case eval output file list
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
